title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). The LVMH chief has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

title: US launches new strikes against Iran after Trump warns country will pay for deaths of American soldiers
Andy Burnham enters Downing Street and appoints former defense minister John Healey as chancellor of the Exchequer
Between China and the US, a new diplomacy of detainees emerges
subhead: Alongside the major issues discussed between the world's two leading powers, the fate of American citizens imprisoned in China on espionage charges has also become a bargaining chip.

title: In southern Lebanon hospital, caregivers strive to overcome traumas of war
subhead: Doctors Without Borders provides individual counseling and group therapy sessions to professionals who have endured four months of conflict and an ongoing, unstable security situation.

title: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
subhead: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

title: Two months before Russian parliamentary elections, antiwar voices still struggle for traction
subhead: The Kremlin continues to stifle the few parliamentary candidates opposed to the war in Ukraine, even as the public and some industrialists begin to imagine peace.

subhead: Le Monde's critics present our selection of exhibitions on painting, contemporary art and photography across France.

title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: The cinematic and audiovisual influence of Homer's 'Odyssey,' from Méliès to Godard
subhead: Homer's ancient Greek epic poem has fueled the imaginations of countless filmmakers, giving rise to a multitude of adaptations, from sword-and-sandal epics to parodies, adult films and even Italian Westerns.
